_doc: Editor steering notes for the conference preview agent. Authoritative — these override prior knowledge and any conflicting context-data interpretation. Keep each note to 1–3 short sentences. Empty strings are dropped silently. Loaded by build_conference_context.py from /cfb-research/writer_notes/<season>/<conf>.json.
_usage: conference_note: 1–3 sentences with two purposes — (a) the meta-narrative for the year (e.g., 'The realignment dust has settled but the talent gap between the top three and the rest is still widening.'), and (b) calibration the agent needs to right-size confidence: what 'good' looks like in this league. SEC example: 'In modern SEC: 7-1 in conf typically reaches the title game; 9-0 has never happened. 9 overall wins is a historic season.' MAC example: 'In the MAC, 8 conference wins is championship caliber; 6-2 contends.' Combine narrative + calibration when both matter.

team_notes.<slug>: 1–3 sentences about that specific team — what the data won't tell the agent (a coaching change, a critical transfer, an injury, an off-field story, the angle the writeup should take).
Slugs match the canonical team slugs in build_team_context.py / scrape_team_context.py.
conference_note: Three storylines define the 2026 Big Ten: (1) the conference has won three straight national titles (Indiana 2025, Ohio State 2024, Michigan 2023) and has caught up to the SEC in the race for top conference, however the Big Ten lacks the depth and still doesn't measure up when comparing the lower half of the conference #8 - #18 team comparison; (2) is brutally top-heavy — Indiana, Oregon, and Ohio State are all preseason top-6 and CFP favorites, while Purdue / Michigan State / Northwestern / Maryland are dreadful; (3) realignment year three — UCLA, USC, Oregon, and Washington are no longer 'new,' the West-coast teams are now regular B1G programs. The Big Ten has played a 9 game conference schedule since 2016 and is the largest conference in FBS with 18 teams. Big Ten tier framework (used in team_notes; tiers describe team caliber, NOT exact record — record varies with schedule strength): Tier 1 = Big Ten title-game favorite if healthy and CFP-bound; Tier 2 = title-game contender, CFP contender; Tier 3 = middle tier and title-game outsider, in the contention mix but outside CFP; Tier 4 = rebuilding year, fighting to get to a bowl game; Tier 5 = down year, where reaching bowl eligibility would itself be the ceiling. When team_notes give a range (e.g., 'Tier 2-3'), the agent should treat the wider boundary as the realistic downside and pick a centering tier in the writeup based on health and early-season tape.

team_notes.illinois: Tier 2-3 Big Ten team. HC Bret Bielema is a great one and a fan favorite for his antics, but mostly his 19 wins the past two seasons.  Illinois has been a mostly forgettable CFB team but Bielema and staff have really elevated the program.  The Illini still have a ceiling of 6 Big Ten wins, 10 overall.  Signed QB Katin Houser from East Carolina who's a good one but defense was torched at times last year and a lot of new faces on offense must gel to keep the momentum going.
team_notes.indiana: Tier 1-2 Big Ten team. The defending national champions after HC Curt Cignetti (27-2 at IU) and staff completed the greatest coaching performance in modern CFB history last year.  Indiana was the losingest CFB program in FBS until Cignetti's arrival.  Indiana has to replace a lot of key pieces including Heisman QB Fernando Mendoza, but Cignetti and his staff are better at evaluating and adding talent than any others in CFB.  Will be a Big Ten co-favorite and a favorite to make the CFP again, as they've done the past two seasons.
team_notes.iowa: Tier 2-3 Big Ten team. HC Kirk Ferentz is CFBs most tenured coach going into his 28th year.  Iowa is famous for great defense and poor offense and is especially poor at passing offense. QBs Jeremy Hecklinski and Hank Brown still battling for QB1 job although neither are projected to be good passers, good game managers at best. Treat QB room as unsettled across the writeup.  Have to replace 17 starters and it's assumed they will to have another decent season, but I'm skeptical.
team_notes.maryland: Tier 4-5 Big Ten team. HC Mike Locksley almost fired after last season and is only 17–48 in Big Ten games. Maryland famously starts strong and then lose nearly all games in October and November like last year going 0-8 starting Oct 4th. QB Malik Washington was a big recruit and played well as a freshman last year.  Have big, talented Defensive Ends and signed the #1 DE recruit coming in this fall in Zion Elee.  Locksley must get Maryland to a bowl or he is likely gone, should have been already.
team_notes.michigan: Tier 2-3 Big Ten team, 2023 National Champions. Previous HC Sherrone Moore was just getting comfortable as Jim Harbaugh's replacement, then was fired after last season for ugly reasons. New HC Kyle Whittingham is a big upgrade.  Whittingham was pushed out of Utah HC job, leaving the Utes as their all time winningest HC. The overall staff is great, OC Jason Beck is a great one as is DC Jay Hill.  QB Underwood enters his 2nd year starting but has not improved as a passer yet, have one of Big Tens best RB duos in Jordan Marshall and five star freshmen RB Savion Hiter. WR has been an issue for a few years, but Andrew Marsh is back and JJ Buchanan from Utah is a good duo.  Defense should be good to great.
team_notes.michigan-state: Tier 4-5 Big Ten team. Hired former Northwestern HC Pat Fitzgerald, Have only won 6 Big Ten games past 3 seasons (6-21 overall) and lost their top QB and WR in the transfer portal after last season.  Rebuilding year at best, Fitzgerald was a great HC but in new NIL era will he adjust?
team_notes.minnesota: Tier 3-4 Big Ten team with a 2 ceiling. HC P. J. Fleck enters his 10th season, has been good, Gophers a consistent bowl team, but have had a hard ceiling. Went 7-0 at home last season and 0-4 on the road (1-1 neutral),  QB Drake Lindsey enters his 2nd year starting and provides excitement, RBs are good with Darius Taylor.  Question is can Fleck break the ceiling?
team_notes.nebraska: Tier 3-4 Big Ten team. One of CFBs most passionate fan bases, Nebraska has not won 8 games or more in a season since 2016, HC Matt Rhule signed big extension last year and then finished 2-5.  Offense has been inconsistent at best and defense has regressed last two years.
team_notes.northwestern: Tier 4-5 Big Ten team. Lack NIL funding but have been decent program, hired OC Chip Kelly to fix offense, brought in Michigan State QB Aiden Chiles from the portal, but he's been inconsistent in his career. HC David Braun has won more games than he should at 19-19 through 3 seasons. Rebuilt home stadium Ryan Field opens October 2nd when Penn State comes to Northwestern.
team_notes.ohio-state: Tier 1 Big Ten team, 2024 National Champions. The most stable CFB program, have won 10 games or more in every season since 2011 (outside of COVID year 2020).  CFP favorite, Big Ten favorite, WR Jeremiah Smith is the best player in CFB, QB Julian Sayin was last years most accurate QB, but the 0-2 finish and loss to Miami in the CFP stung.  HC Ryan Day great, and underappreciated.
team_notes.oregon: Tier 1-2 Big Ten team. Joined Big Ten prior to the 2024 season, then won the Big Ten in their first season. Have been blown out of last two College Football playoffs leaving a feeling of wanting more. QB Dante Moore turned down NFL to come back, Heisman contender, Offense should be great if OL rebuilds, Defense is good but flopped on big stages.
team_notes.penn-state: Tier 2-3 Big Ten team. Fired James Franklin in October last year, hired Matt Campbell from Iowa State.  Campbell brought his OC and 24 portal players with him, national media have jokingly referred to Penn State as Iowa State East right now.  Campbell is good coach, won at ISU with less talent than he can acquire at Penn State.
team_notes.purdue: Tier 4-5 Big Ten team. Worst team in conference, haven't won a conference game since 2023, hired a good coach in Barry Odom prior to last season but still went 2-10 overall.  Purdue has not adapted to the NIL era, or won't.  Odom needs to win some games to provide any hope.
team_notes.rutgers: Tier 4-5 Big Ten team. Have been a decent program under Greg Schiano, but haven't kept up in NIL era with roster funding.  Went to a bowl in 2023 & 2024, just missed last year going 5-7 which may signal a downward trend. Seem to find ways to build an Offense but defense has been a big issue.
team_notes.ucla: Tier 3-4 Big Ten team. Joined Big Ten prior to the 2024 season.  Fired HC DeShaun Foster last year in mid September, hired Bob Chesney after season completed.  Chesney is a good coach from James Madison, like Curt Cignetti at Indiana.  UCLA fans have been uninterested lately, the Chesney hire is the first sign the administration has seemingly cared in a while as well.  Genuine excitement for the first time in many years.
team_notes.usc: Tier 2-3 Big Ten team. Joined Big Ten prior to the 2024 season.  HC Lincoln Riley is on the hot seat as his tenure hasn't lived up to the hype, Trojan fans expect better than just 11-7 in Big Ten play so far.  Defense is a consistent issue, was improving under DC D’Anton Lynn but he left to go to Penn State after last season. Former TCU HC Gary Patterson was hired as new DC.  QB Jayden Maiava is a good one, but has less weapons as they downgrade at wide receiver as USC lost top 2 from last year who will be difficult to replace.  Riley needs to at least threaten the CFP to keep fans off his back, although they just signed the #1 recruiting class coming in this season.
team_notes.washington: Tier 2-3 Big Ten team. Joined Big Ten prior to the 2024 season. QB Demond Williams tried to leave via portal and was coerced back making the vibes awkward for what's supposed to be a team leader. HC Jedd Fisch is a good one entering year three after having won 6 games in his first season and 9 games in his second.  Huskies lost top RB & WR from last season, RBs are a question after missing spring with injuries.  DC Ryan Walters a good one, defense has been solid and will be good again this year.
team_notes.wisconsin: Tier 4-5 Big Ten team. Offense has been poor under HC Luke Fickell & he was almost fired last year as his tenure has gotten worse each of his previous 3 years. QB Colten Joseph, a portal addition from Old Dominion, is a good QB which has been a huge issue for Wisconsin. Did the Badgers get enough to support him is a big question, although they don't play many of the top Big Ten teams this fall so they're record should improve.
